title: selliguea subfasciata
intro: Selliguea subfasciata, also known as Polypodium subfasciatum, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Selliguea subfasciata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat a range of conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
